>been trying to find jobs a few months before that cuz I realized there was no pleasing him
>haven't found any jobs, its unreal how bad the market is, have this bad feeling its never going to get better
>PIP ends on Tuesday
I am an engineer. I started off as a tech and worked my ass off getting bachelors full time to escape manual labor. Now im gonna be that goy with a masters degree who took a 500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work. I honestly feel like god did this beacuse its too much bad things happening at once that are culminating into my total demise.

Early replies (first 3)

62388691
>>62388676
>500 percent pay cut to do repetitive ligament destroying work
Trades pay more than ever, especially vs pajeet-saturated office work. Skill issue

62388694
>>62388676
My entire life has been nothing but fighting tooth and nail, working myself 1000x harder than anyone anywhere ever for 0.001% of what anyone else gets for free. I have felt that god is purposely shitting on me pretty much my whole life. It doesn't get better, it only ever gets worse. If anything happens to me that seem to be a good thing, I have 6 months max to enjoy it because that'll be taken from me and then I'll be beaten up and crushed down for added effect. It's a real comedy out there.

So don't worry bro, you have a lot of time ahead of you to suffer far more. If there really is a god, he must be the most perfectly evil of entities.

62388697
The worst part is that in my meeting with my boss today he didnt mention if I will pass the PIP. He said he needed to talk with some people including HR. So 90 percent chance he is going to fire me but just enough chance he wont that I have to spend the weekend waiting instead of processing the pain. And if he doesnt fire me, I will be a totally buckbroken employee who will never care about a rasie and work 60 hours weeks. Like a absurd housewife that has no were else to go and she tries to esapce and gets rapped(applies to ghost jobs) even worse so she comes back to him. Thats me. I used to think my relationship with work was like between a man and a woman, were I am the man that picks and chooses women and is the one who leaves but its all turned around past couple of years. I am nothing but a side pieace female whore to these companies now, they treat me like a contract worker without paying me like one. They got us with not giving raises to match inflation, they got us with return to work mandates and now, they are going to get us by firing us and leaving us with nothing. U will get put on a PIP too, at the worst time possible also. They wont even lay u off anymore, they want to save a little bit of money by denying us unemployment insurance by firing us through a PIP.


Was being in ur 30s a good excperince back in the day? There is no way human civilization would have gotten to were it is if it was always this bad for everyone. Of course, I am a faggot and just suck but the fsct of the matter is that PIPs and no jobs has been a very common trope on the internet lately

item.title: Canadian Mortgages are insane — 54 replies — 216.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Canadian Mortgages are insane

Open thread • Replies: 54 • Updated: 2026-06-20 01:45 UTC

OP

Anyone that bought homes over the last 10 years has no concept of the magnitude of the level of debt they have.

Imagine 400k to 900k mortgages on cookie cutter homes with no space between them. This is just fine in the mind of the average leaf.

Let me be clear, they are never going to pay it off. Never. They think because everyone else has a big ass mortgage that its fine. It took me 17 years just to pay down my 228k mortgage to 68k where it is now. How the fuck do these people think at 38 with a 500k mortgage they can pay it down by the time they retire? LMAO.

Fucking totally disconnected from reality. Millions of us.

>I work with commercial mortgages professionally
Oh look, a guy who sells debt for a living thinks you should be in more debt. Shocker. Enjoy bankruptcy as the commercial real estate market craters from remote work.

Nothing wrong with paying down debt. Interest is a guaranteed loss. Investing is a speculative gain. There is a solid argument that getting rid of any amount of guaranteed loss is better than a non-guaranteed gain.

39$/hour as a masonry laborer.

I started at 25$/hr in the company’s office, where I used my tech skills to build website stuff, learned and ran Google Ads and got some work for the company, which progressively rewarded me with raises.

I ran out of work at some point due to it being a small company so I was put in the field as a laborer. Now I do grunt work but get paid the same.

Although I’m not “growing”, this job feels like most feasible path for me to build my own construction company. I’ve already done jobs on the side and have my LLC open, website, SEO going on.

I want to scale my company to 10-15k/month net, but have been running into lots of cheap people when estimating jobs. It is a game of consistency, and sooner rather than later I can see my GBP with many reviews bringing me an organic pipeline of jobs, as well as eventual institutional/commercial contacts I meet here and there giving me consistent and higher margin work.
